Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 1, 070+ [Šumma Izbu 14][via cams/gkab]

oo NaN  (missing)
Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

[...] [ki]-ma [...]

(r 1') [...] like [...]

r 2'2'

[...] BI SU ḪA [...]

(r 2') [...] ... [...]

r 3'3'

[...] [ki]-ma na-⸢ru?-qu?⸣ x x [(x)] SU ḪA x [...]

(r 3') [...] like a leather bag ... [...] ...

r 4'4'

[...] x ki-ma na-ru-[qi₂?] ⸢KI?⸣ GA SU ḪA ⸢UZ?⸣ MA [...]

(r 4') [...] like a leather bag ... [...] ... [...]

r 5'5'

[...] x na-ru-qi₂ LIMMU₂-<<MEŠ>> la₃ ⸢TUKU?⸣ [...] x x x [...]

(r 5') [...] ... a leather bag, it does not have four [...] ... [...]

r 6'6'

[x] iz-[bu] [...] ša₂ 15 kaṣ₃-ṣa-at EREN₂ NUN [...]

(r 6') [...] the right [...] is trimmed: the princes's army [...].

r 7'7'

BE iz-bu GIR₃ [(x)] ⸢2⸣.30 kaṣ₃-ṣa-at EREN₂ KUR₂ [...]

(r 7') If an anomaly's left foot [...] is trimmed: the enemy's army [...].

r 8'8'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ [2].30 kaṣ₃-ṣa ⸢RU?⸣ x [...]

(r 8') If an anomaly's left feet [...] are trimmed ... [...].

r 9'9'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ x [x]-MEŠ kaṣ₃-ṣa EGIR x [...]

(r 9') If an anomaly's feet ... [...] are trimmed: behind ... [...].

r 10'10'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-ME-šu₂ LIMMU₂ x [x] kaṣ₃-ṣa KUR₂ LU₂ [...]

(r 10') If the four feet of an anomaly ... [...] are trimmed: the man's enemy [...].

r 11'11'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-šu₂ ša₂ 15 ku-ri-a-at lu₂KUR₂!(KUR) NUN ina ki-ši₂-it-ti-šu₂ ⸢UŠ?⸣ [...]

(r 11') If an anomaly's right foot is short: the enemy will pursue the prince during his conquest [...].

r 12'12'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-šu₂ ša₂ 2.30 ku-ri-a-at NUN lu₂KUR₂ ina ki-ši₂-it-ti-šu₂ ⸢UŠ!⸣ [...]

(r 12') If an anomaly's left foot is short: the prince will pursue the enemy during his conquest [...].

r 13'13'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-<MEŠ>-šu₂ IGI-MEŠ LUGUD₂.DA-MEŠ [x] ni-iš ŠU-MIN-šu₂ KI BAR UL MA ḪIR [...]

(r 13') If an anomaly's front legs are short: hand-lifting prayer ... [...].

r 14'14'

A ŠU₂ x x ⸢RU?⸣ DAG? [...] NUN IN [...]

r 15'15'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ EGIR-MEŠ LUGUD₂.⸢DA⸣ [x] ŠU₂-MEŠ BAL LUGAL [...]

(r 15') If an anomaly's rear legs are short: ... the king's reign [...].

r 16'16'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ ⸢NI?⸣ IS ta-⸢ar?⸣-x [x] LUGUD₂.DA-MEŠ BAL LUGAL [...]

(r 16') If an anomaly's feet ... are short: the king's reign [...].

r 17'17'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-šu₂ ša₂ 15 GID₂.DA-ma ina ⸢kin?⸣-[ṣi]-šu₂ KI i-kab-ba-as x [...]

(r 17') If an anomaly's right foot is long and it is treading the ground with its shins: [...].

r 18'18'

BE iz-bu [...] ša₂ 2.30 GID₂.DA-ma ina kin-[ṣi-šu₂] x KI i-kab-ba-as ⸢RU?⸣ [...]

(r 18') If an anomaly left (foot) is long and it is treading the ground with its shins: ... [...].

r 19'19'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-[šu₂] ⸢IGI⸣-MEŠ GID₂.DA-ma ⸢kin?⸣-[ṣi-šu₂] [x] gišTUKUL LUGAL x [...]

(r 19') If an anomaly's front feet are long and its shins [...]: the king's weapon ... [...].

r 20'20'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-⸢šu₂⸣ [...] EGIR E₂ [...]

(r 20') If an anomaly's feet [...] behind the house [...].

r 21'21'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ x [...]

(r 21') If an anomaly's feet ... [...].

r 22'22'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-šu₂ IGItu₄ [...]

(r 22') If an anomaly's front foot [...].

r 23'23'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-šu₂ IGItu₄ ša₂ [...]

(r 23') If an anomaly's front ... foot [...].

r 24'24'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ IGItu₄ ⸢RU?⸣ [...]

(r 24') If an anomaly's front feet ... [...].

r 25'25'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-⸢šu₂ EGIR⸣tu₄ ⸢ša₂⸣ [...]

(r 25') If an anomaly's rear ... foot [...].

r 26'26'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-šu₂ ⸢EGIR⸣tu₄ ša₂ x [...]

(r 26') If an anomaly's rear ... foot [...].

r 27'27'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-[šu₂] ⸢EGIR⸣tu₄ SUḪUŠ [...]

(r 27') If an anomaly's rear feet: the foundations [...].

r 28'28'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-[šu₂] [x] BA x [...]

(r 28') If an anomaly's feet ... [...].

r 29'29'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ [x] BA [x] ŠA₃-šu₂ x [...]

(r 29') If an anomaly's feet ... its belly ... [...].

r 30'30'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-[šu₂] 15 ⸢ana ki-di pa⸣-as-lat x [...]

(r 30') If an anomaly's right feet is twisted outwards ... [...].

r 31'31'

BE iz-bu ⸢GIR₃-šu₂⸣ 2.30 ana ki-di pa-⸢as⸣-lat ⸢TUR₃?⸣ [...]

(r 31') If an anomaly's left foot is twisted outwards: the animal stall [...].

r 32'32'

BE iz-bu ⸢GIR₃⸣-ME-šu₂ IGI-MEŠ ana ki-di pa-as-la E₂ BI ⸢BIR?⸣ [...]

(r 32') If an anomaly's front feet are twisted outwards: that household will be scattered [...].

r 33'33'

BE iz-bu ⸢GIR₃⸣-ME-šu₂ EGIR-ME ana ki-di [pa]-as-la EGIR ⸢E₂⸣ [...]

(r 33') If an anomaly's rear feet are twisted outwards: the rear of the house [...].

r 34'34'

BE iz-bu ⸢GIR₃⸣-ME-šu₂ 4-BA ana ki-di pa-⸢as⸣-la EN dan-nu ZI-ma KUR u₂-⸢šam⸣-[qat] [...]

(r 34') If an anomaly's four feet are twisted outwards: a mighty lord will attack and will provoke the fall of the country.

r 35'35'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-ME ša₂ 15 ana ku-tum₃ ŠA₃ GURar₂ TUR₃ BI UKU₂in : BE iz-bu x [...]

(r 35') If an anomaly's right feet turn towards the belly: that animal stall will become poor. : If an anomaly ... [...].

r 36'36'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ IGItu₄ ana ku-tum₃ ŠA₃ GUR-MEŠ TUR₃ BI BIR : BE iz-bu GIR₃-ME [...]

(r 36') If an anomaly's front feet turn towards the belly: that animal stall will be scattered. : If an anomaly's feet [...].

r 37'37'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ 4-BA ana ku-tum₃ ŠA₃ GUR-MEŠ KUR ⸢BAL?⸣ [...]

(r 37') If an anomaly's four legs turn towards the belly: the land will revolt [...].

r 38'38'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ ana pa-pa-an ŠA₃-šu₂ GUR-⸢MEŠ SU?-šu₂?⸣ [...]

(r 38') If an anomaly's feet turn towards the diaphragm: its body [...]

r 39'39'

BE iz-bu 2 GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ ana IGI-šu₂ ša₂-ni-tu₄ ana EGIR-šu₂ GARat x [...]

(r 39') If an anomaly (has) two feet on its front, another is located on its rear ... [...].

r 40'40'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ 4-BA ana IGI-šu₂ GUR-MEŠ ḪI [...]

(r 40') If an anomaly's four feet turn towards its front ... [...].

r 41'41'

BE iz-bu GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ 4-BA ana EGIR-šu₂ GUR-MEŠ u pa-nu-šu₂ GAR [...]

(r 41') If an anomaly's four feet turn towards its rear and its face is located [...].

r 42'42'

[...] 15 NIGIN-ma ana IGI KUN-šu₂ UMBIN-šu₂ [...]

(r 42') [...] circles on the right and its claw [...] in front of its tail [...].

r 43'43'

[...] x [...] ⸢IM?⸣ 15-KAM₂ BE MUNUS ⸢PEŠ₄⸣ [...]

(r 43') [...] ... [...] Tablet 15: "If a woman is pregnant [...].

(rest of reverse missing)
